columns.description: The AlertSeverityText refers to the text description associated with a specific alert or notification severity level. It provides a detailed explanation of the severity level and is used in various SAP transactions and reports.

columns.name: AlertValue
columns.description: The AlertValue represents the importance of the message of a log attribute, characterized by color and severity.

columns.name: AuditClassId
columns.description: The AuditClassId is a numeric identifier used to classify different types of audit events in SAP. It helps group similar events together and is used in various transactions and reports to provide information about audit events and to help with auditing and compliance requirements.

columns.name: _BilledSize
columns.description: The record size in bytes

columns.name: ClientId
columns.description: The ClientId is a three-digit number that identifies a specific client within an SAP system. The ClientId is used in various SAP transactions and configuration settings to identify the client and ensure that the correct client is being accessed or configured.

columns.name: Computer
columns.description: The Computer refers to the name or IP address of the user's machine. It is used to identify the machine from which a specific activity or event was performed.

columns.name: Email
columns.description: The Email refers to the email address associated with a specific user. It is used to identify the user via email within the SAP system.

columns.name: Host
columns.description: The Host refers to the name of the computer or server on which the SAP system is running. It is used to identify and locate the SAP system within a network.

columns.name: Instance
columns.description: The Instance refers to a specific installation of an SAP system on a server. An SAP system can have multiple instances running on the same server, each with its own set of processes and resources. The Instance is identified by a unique name that is set during the installation of the SAP system, following syntax: <HOST><SYSID><SYSNR>.

columns.name: _IsBillable

columns.name: MessageContainerId
columns.description: The MessageContainerId is a unique identifier for a specific message container, which is a logical grouping of related messages or log entries within an SAP system.

columns.name: MessageId
columns.description: The MessageId is a unique identifier for a specific message or log entry used to identify and track messages and log entries within an SAP system.

columns.name: MessageText
columns.description: The MessageText is the text of a specific message or log entry. It provides information about the event or issue that is being logged and is used in various SAP transactions and reports to provide context and details about specific messages or log entries.

columns.name: MonitoringObjectName
columns.description: The MonitoringObjectName is the name of the object being monitored by a specific monitoring activity. It is used in various SAP monitoring tools and reports to provide information about the status and performance of the monitored object.

columns.name: MonitorShortName

columns.name: SalTimeChar6

columns.name: SapProcessType
columns.description: The SapProcessType refers to the type of process that is being executed within the SAP system. This can include various types of processes such as background jobs, dialog processes, or update processes.

columns.description: The SapWorkProcessName refers to the name of a specific work process within the SAP system used to identify and monitor specific work processes. It is typically a unique alphanumeric code.

columns.description: The DNS authenticated answer flag, which is related to DNSSEC, indicates in a response that all data included in the answer and authority sections of the response have been verified by the server according to the policies of that server. see RFC 3655 Section 6.1 for more information.

columns.description: The DNS authoritative answer flag indicates whether the response from the server was authoritative.

columns.description: The DNS CD flag, which is related to DNSSEC, indicates in a query that non-verified data is acceptable to the system sending the query.

columns.name: DnsFlagsRecursionAvailable
columns.description: The DNS RA flag indicates in a response that that server supports recursive queries.

columns.name: DnsFlagsRecursionDesired
columns.description: The DNS recursion desired flag indicates in a request that that client would like the server to use recursive queries.

columns.name: DnsFlagsTruncated
columns.description: The DNS TC flag indicates that a response was truncates as it exceeded the maximum response size.

columns.name: DnsFlagsZ

columns.name: TcpFlagsAck
columns.description: The TCP ACK flag reported. The acknowledgment flag is used to acknowledge the successful receipt of a packet. As we can see from the diagram above, the receiver sends an ACK as well as a SYN in the second step of the three way handshake process to tell the sender that it received its initial packet.

columns.name: TcpFlagsFin
columns.description: The TCP FIN flag reported. The finished flag means there is no more data from the sender. Therefore, it is used in the last packet sent from the sender.

columns.name: TcpFlagsPsh
columns.description: The TCP PSH flag reported. The push flag is somewhat similar to the URG flag and tells the receiver to process these packets as they are received instead of buffering them.

columns.name: TcpFlagsRst
columns.description: The TCP RST flag reported. The reset flag gets sent from the receiver to the sender when a packet is sent to a particular host that was not expecting it.

columns.name: TcpFlagsSyn
columns.description: The TCP SYN flag reported. The synchronisation flag is used as a first step in establishing a three way handshake between two hosts. Only the first packet from both the sender and receiver should have this flag set.

columns.name: TcpFlagsUrg
columns.description: The TCP URG flag reported. The urgent flag is used to notify the receiver to process the urgent packets before processing all other packets. The receiver will be notified when all known urgent data has been received. See RFC 6093 for more details.

columns.description: The confidence level of the threat identified, normalized to a value between 0 and a 100.

columns.name: ThreatField
columns.description: The field for which a threat was identified. The value is either SrcIpAddr, DstIpAddr, Domain, or DnsResponseName.

columns.name: ThreatFirstReportedTime
columns.description: The first time the IP address or domain were identified as a threat.

columns.name: ThreatId
columns.description: The ID of the threat or malware identified in the network session.

columns.name: ThreatIpAddr
columns.description: An IP address for which a threat was identified. The field ThreatField contains the name of the field ThreatIpAddr represents.

columns.name: ThreatIsActive
columns.description: True ID the threat identified is considered an active threat.

columns.name: ThreatLastReportedTime
columns.description: The last time the IP address or domain were identified as a threat.

columns.name: ThreatName
columns.description: The name of the threat or malware identified in the network session.

columns.name: ThreatOriginalConfidence
columns.description: The original confidence level of the threat identified, as reported by the reporting device.

columns.name: ThreatOriginalRiskLevel
columns.description: The risk level as reported by the reporting device.

columns.name: ThreatRiskLevel
columns.description: The risk level associated with the session. The level is a number between 0 to 100.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The timestamp (UTC) reflecting the time in which the event was generated.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

columns.name: AdditionalEventData
columns.description: Additional data about the event that was not part of the request or response.

columns.name: APIVersion
columns.description: Identifies the API version associated with the AwsApiCall eventType value.

columns.name: AwsEventId
columns.description: GUID generated by CloudTrail to uniquely identify each event. You can use this value to identify a single event.

columns.name: AWSRegion
columns.description: The AWS region that the request was made to.

columns.name: AwsRequestId
columns.description: deprecated, please use AwsRequestId_ instead.

columns.name: AwsRequestId_
columns.description: The value that identifies the request. The service being called generates this value.

columns.name: _BilledSize
columns.description: The record size in bytes

columns.name: Category
columns.description: Shows the event category that is used in LookupEvents calls.

columns.name: CidrIp
columns.description: The CIDR IP is located under RequestParameters in CloudTrail, and it is used to specify the IP permissions for a security group rule. The IPv4 CIDR range.

columns.name: CipherSuite
columns.description: Optional. Part of tlsDetails. The cipher suite (combination of security algorithms used) of a request.

columns.name: ClientProvidedHostHeader
columns.description: Optional. Part of tlsDetails. The client-provided host name used in the service API call, which is typically the FQDN of the service endpoint.

columns.name: DestinationPort
columns.description: The DestinationPort is located under RequestParameters in CloudTrail, and it is used to specify the IP permissions for a security group rule. The end of port range for the TCP and UDP protocols, or an ICMP code.

columns.name: EC2RoleDelivery
columns.description: The friendly name of the user or role that issued the session.

columns.name: ErrorCode
columns.description: The AWS service error if the request returns an error.

columns.name: ErrorMessage
columns.description: The error description when available. This message includes messages for authorization failures. CloudTrail captures the message logged by the service in its exception handling.

columns.name: EventName
columns.description: The requested action, which is one of the actions in the API for that service.

columns.name: EventSource
columns.description: The service that the request was made to. This name is typically a short form of the service name without spaces plus .amazonaws.com.

columns.name: EventTypeName
columns.description: Identifies the type of event that generated the event record. This can be the one of the following values: AwsApiCall, AwsServiceEvent, AwsConsoleAction , AwsConsoleSignIn.

columns.name: EventVersion
columns.description: The version of the log event format.

columns.name: IpProtocol
columns.description: The IP protocol is located under RequestParameters in CloudTrail, and it is used to specify the IP permissions for a security group rule. The IP protocol name or number. The valid values are tcp, udp, icmp, or a protocol number.

columns.name: _IsBillable
columns.description: Specifies whether ingesting the data is billable. When _IsBillable isfalseingestion isn't billed to your Azure account

columns.name: ManagementEvent
columns.description: A Boolean value that identifies whether the event is a management event.

columns.name: OperationName
columns.description: Constant value: CloudTrail.

columns.name: ReadOnly
columns.description: Identifies whether this operation is a read-only operation.

columns.name: RecipientAccountId
columns.description: Represents the account ID that received this event. The recipientAccountID may be different from the CloudTrail userIdentity Element accountId. This can occur in cross-account resource access.

columns.name: RequestParameters
columns.description: The parameters, if any, that were sent with the request. These parameters are documented in the API reference documentation for the appropriate AWS service.

columns.name: Resources
columns.description: A list of resources accessed in the event.

columns.name: ResponseElements
columns.description: The response element for actions that make changes (create, update, or delete actions). If an action does not change state (for example, a request to get or list objects), this element is omitted.

columns.name: ServiceEventDetails
columns.description: Identifies the service event, including what triggered the event and the result.

columns.name: SessionCreationDate
columns.description: The date and time when the temporary security credentials were issued.

columns.name: SessionIssuerAccountId
columns.description: The account that owns the entity that was used to get credentials.

columns.name: SessionIssuerArn
columns.description: The ARN of the source (account, IAM user, or role) that was used to get temporary security credentials.

columns.name: SessionIssuerPrincipalId
columns.description: The internal ID of the entity that was used to get credentials.

columns.name: SessionIssuerType
columns.description: The source of the temporary security credentials, such as Root, IAMUser, or Role.

columns.name: SessionIssuerUserName
columns.description: The friendly name of the user or role that issued the session.

columns.name: SessionMfaAuthenticated
columns.description: The value is true if the root user or IAM user whose credentials were used for the request also was authenticated with an MFA device; otherwise, false.

columns.name: SharedEventId
columns.description: GUID generated by CloudTrail to uniquely identify CloudTrail events from the same AWS action that is sent to different AWS accounts.

columns.name: SourceIpAddress
columns.description: The IP address that the request was made from. For actions that originate from the service console, the address reported is for the underlying customer resource, not the console web server. For services in AWS, only the DNS name is displayed.

columns.name: SourcePort
columns.description: The SourcePort is located under RequestParameters in CloudTrail, and it is used to specify the IP permissions for a security group rule. The start of port range for the TCP and UDP protocols, or an ICMP type number.

columns.name: SourceSystem
columns.description: The type of agent the event was collected by. For example,OpsManagerfor Windows agent, either direct connect or Operations Manager,Linuxfor all Linux agents, orAzurefor Azure Diagnostics

columns.name: TenantId
columns.description: The Log Analytics workspace ID

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The timestamp (UTC). An event's time stamp comes from the local host that provides the service API endpoint on which the API call was made.

columns.name: TlsVersion
columns.description: Optional. Part of tlsDetails. The TLS version of a request.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

columns.name: UserAgent
columns.description: The agent through which the request was made, such as the AWS Management Console, an AWS service, the AWS SDKs or the AWS CLI.

columns.name: UserIdentityAccessKeyId
columns.description: The access key ID that was used to sign the request.

columns.name: UserIdentityAccountId
columns.description: The account that owns the entity that granted permissions for the request.

columns.name: UserIdentityArn
columns.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the principal that made the call.

columns.name: UserIdentityInvokedBy
columns.description: The name of the AWS service that made the request.

columns.name: UserIdentityPrincipalid
columns.description: A unique identifier for the entity that made the call.

columns.name: UserIdentityStoreArn
columns.description: ARN of the identity store (e.g., IAM Identity Center/SSO directory) from which the user identity originates.

columns.name: UserIdentityType
columns.description: The type of the identity. The following values are possible: Root, IAMUser, AssumedRole, FederatedUser, Directory, AWSAccount, AWSService, Unknown.

columns.name: UserIdentityUserId
columns.description: Unique internal AWS identifier of the IAM entity (user, role, or federated identity) that performed the action.

columns.name: UserIdentityUserName
columns.description: The name of the identity that made the call.

columns.name: VpcEndpointId
columns.description: Identifies the VPC endpoint in which requests were made from a VPC to another AWS service.

columns.name: _BilledSize
columns.description: The record size in bytes

columns.name: ExtractedTime
columns.description: The timestamp (UTC) of when the event was generated.

columns.name: _IsBillable
columns.description: Specifies whether ingesting the data is billable. When _IsBillable isfalseingestion isn't billed to your Azure account

columns.name: Message
columns.description: The data contained within logs from CloudWatch.

columns.name: SourceSystem
columns.description: The type of agent the event was collected by. For example,OpsManagerfor Windows agent, either direct connect or Operations Manager,Linuxfor all Linux agents, orAzurefor Azure Diagnostics

columns.name: TenantId
columns.description: The Log Analytics workspace ID

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The timestamp (UTC) when the event was generated and equals to 'ExtractedTime' when included in message. If timestamp is missing, it's set to the ingestion time.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

columns.name: AccountId
columns.description: The AWS account ID of the owner of the source network interface for which traffic is recorded. If the network interface is created by an AWS service, for example when creating a VPC endpoint or Network Load Balancer, the record may display unknown for this field.

columns.name: ActivityType
columns.description: A formatted string representing the type of activity that triggered the finding.

columns.name: Arn
columns.description: Amazon resource name of the finding.

columns.name: _BilledSize
columns.description: The record size in bytes

columns.name: Description
columns.description: Description of the primary purpose of the threat or attack related to the finding.

columns.name: Id
columns.description: A unique Finding ID for this finding type and set of parameters. New occurrences of activity matching this pattern will be aggregated to the same ID.

columns.name: _IsBillable
columns.description: Specifies whether ingesting the data is billable. When _IsBillable isfalseingestion isn't billed to your Azure account

columns.name: Partition
columns.description: The AWS partition in which the finding was generated.

columns.name: Region
columns.description: The AWS region in which the finding was generated.

columns.name: ResourceDetails
columns.description: Gives details on the AWS resource that was targeted by the trigger activity. The information available varies based on resource type and action typ.

columns.name: SchemaVersion
columns.description: The Guard Duty finding version.

columns.name: ServiceDetails
columns.description: Gives details on the AWS service that was related to the finding, including Action, Actor/Target, Evidence, Anomalous behavior and Additional information.

columns.name: Severity
columns.description: A finding's assigned severity level of either High, Medium, or Low.

columns.name: SourceSystem
columns.description: The type of agent the event was collected by. For example,OpsManagerfor Windows agent, either direct connect or Operations Manager,Linuxfor all Linux agents, orAzurefor Azure Diagnostics

columns.name: TenantId
columns.description: The Log Analytics workspace ID

columns.name: TimeCreated
columns.description: The time and date when this finding was first created. If this value differs from Updated at (TimeGenerated), it indicates that the activity has occurred multiple times and is an ongoing issue.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The timestamp (UTC) of when the event was generated, The last time this finding was updated with new activity matching the pattern that prompted GuardDuty to generate this finding.

columns.name: Title
columns.description: Summary of the primary purpose of the threat or attack related to the finding.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

columns.name: Ack
columns.description: Indicates whether the ACK flag is set in the TCP packet (true/false).

columns.name: AppProto
columns.description: The application layer protocol detected (e.g., HTTP, HTTPS, DNS).

columns.name: AvailabilityZone
columns.description: The AWS Availability Zone where the firewall instance is located.

columns.name: _BilledSize
columns.description: The record size in bytes

columns.name: DestIp
columns.description: The destination IP address of the packet.

columns.name: DestPort
columns.description: The destination port to which the packet was sent.

columns.name: Ecn
columns.description: Indicates whether the ECN flag is set in the TCP packet (true/false).

columns.name: EventTimestamp
columns.description: The epoch timestamp of when the event occurred.

columns.name: EventType
columns.description: The type of event recorded (e.g., flow, alert, drop, pass).

columns.name: Fin
columns.description: Indicates whether the FIN flag is set in the TCP packet (true/false).

columns.name: FirewallName
columns.description: The name of the AWS Network Firewall instance generating the log.

columns.name: FlowId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the network flow related to this event.

columns.name: _IsBillable
columns.description: Specifies whether ingesting the data is billable. When _IsBillable isfalseingestion isn't billed to your Azure account

columns.name: NetFlowAge
columns.description: The duration of the network flow in seconds.

columns.name: NetFlowBytes
columns.description: The total number of bytes transferred in the network flow.

columns.name: NetFlowEnd
columns.description: The timestamp when the network flow ended.

columns.name: NetFlowMaxttl
columns.description: The maximum Time-to-Live (TTL) observed in the network flow.

columns.name: NetFlowMinttl
columns.description: The minimum Time-to-Live (TTL) observed in the network flow.

columns.name: NetFlowPkts
columns.description: The number of packets in the network flow.

columns.name: NetFlowStart
columns.description: The timestamp when the network flow started.

columns.name: Proto
columns.description: The protocol used (e.g., TCP, UDP, ICMP).

columns.name: Psh
columns.description: Indicates whether the PSH flag is set in the TCP packet (true/false).

columns.name: Rst
columns.description: Indicates whether the RST flag is set in the TCP packet (true/false).

columns.name: SourceSystem
columns.description: The type of agent the event was collected by. For example,OpsManagerfor Windows agent, either direct connect or Operations Manager,Linuxfor all Linux agents, orAzurefor Azure Diagnostics

columns.name: SrcIp
columns.description: The source IP address of the packet that triggered the event.

columns.name: SrcPort
columns.description: The source port from which the packet originated.

columns.name: Syn
columns.description: Indicates whether the SYN flag is set in the TCP packet (true/false).

columns.name: TCPFlags
columns.description: The TCP flags observed in the packet

columns.description: The Log Analytics workspace ID

columns.name: TimeGenerated

columns.description: The exact timestamp when the event was captured.

columns.name: AccountDisplayName

columns.name: AccountId

columns.name: AccountObjectId

columns.name: AccountType

columns.description: Type of activity that triggered the event

columns.name: CountryCode
columns.description: Two-letter code indicating the country where the client IP address is geolocated

columns.name: DeviceType
columns.description: Type of device based on purpose and functionality, such as network device, workstation, server, mobile, gaming console, or printer

columns.name: IPAddress
columns.description: IP address assigned to the device during communication

columns.name: IPCategory
columns.description: Additional information about the IP address

columns.name: IPTags
columns.description: Customer-defined information applied to specific IP addresses and IP address ranges

columns.name: IsAdminOperation
columns.description: Indicates whether the activity was performed by an administrator

columns.name: IsAnonymousProxy
columns.description: Indicates whether the IP address belongs to a known anonymous proxy

columns.name: _IsBillable
columns.description: Specifies whether ingesting the data is billable. When _IsBillable isfalseingestion isn't billed to your Azure account

columns.name: IsExternalUser
columns.description: Indicates whether a user inside the network doesn't belong to the organization's domain

columns.name: IsImpersonated

columns.name: ISP
columns.description: Internet service provider associated with the IP address

columns.name: LastSeenForUser
columns.description: Number of days since each statistical feature for the user was last seen

columns.name: OAuthAppId

columns.name: ObjectId
columns.description: Unique identifier of the object that the recorded action was applied to

columns.name: ObjectName
columns.description: Name of the object that the recorded action was applied to

columns.name: ObjectType
columns.description: The type of object, such as a file or a folder, that the recorded action was applied to

columns.name: OSPlatform
columns.description: Platform of the operating system running on the device. This indicates specific operating systems, including variations within the same family, such as Windows 10 and Windows 7

columns.name: RawEventData
columns.description: Raw event information from the source application or service in JSON format

columns.name: ReportId
columns.description: Unique identifier for the event

columns.name: SessionData
columns.description: Session identifiers (if provided by the audit source)

columns.name: SourceSystem
columns.description: The type of agent the event was collected by. For example,OpsManagerfor Windows agent, either direct connect or Operations Manager,Linuxfor all Linux agents, orAzurefor Azure Diagnostics

columns.description: Date and time (UTC) when the record was generated

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

columns.name: UncommonForUser
columns.description: List of features observed to be statistically uncommon for the user that performed the activity

columns.name: UserAgent
columns.description: User agent information from the web browser or other client application

columns.name: UserAgentTags
columns.description: More information provided by Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps in a tag in the user agent field. Can have any of the following values: Native client, Outdated browser, Outdated operating system, Robot

columns.name: Activity
columns.description: A string that represents a human-readable and understandable description of the event.

columns.name: AdditionalExtensions
columns.description: A placeholder for additional fields. Fields are logged as key-value pairs.

columns.name: ApplicationProtocol
columns.description: The protocol used in the application, such as HTTP, HTTPS, SSHv2, Telnet, POP, IMPA, IMAPS, and so on.

columns.name: _BilledSize
columns.description: The record size in bytes

columns.name: CollectorHostName
columns.description: The hostname of the collector machine running the agent.

columns.name: CommunicationDirection
columns.description: Any information about the direction the observed communication has taken. Valid values: 0 = Inbound, 1 = Outbound.

columns.name: Computer
columns.description: Host, from Syslog.

columns.name: DestinationDnsDomain
columns.description: The DNS part of the fully-qualified domain name (FQDN).

columns.name: DestinationHostName
columns.description: The destination that the event refers to in an IP network. The format should be an FQDN associated with the destination node, when a node is available. For example: host.domain.com or host.

columns.name: DestinationIP
columns.description: The destination IpV4 address that the event refers to in an IP network.

columns.name: DestinationMACAddress
columns.description: The destination MAC address (FQDN).

columns.name: DestinationNTDomain
columns.description: The Windows domain name of the destination address.

columns.name: DestinationPort
columns.description: Destination port. Valid values: 0 - 65535.

columns.name: DestinationProcessId
columns.description: The ID of the destination process associated with the event.

columns.name: DestinationProcessName
columns.description: The name of the event's destination process, such as telnetd or sshd.

columns.name: DestinationServiceName
columns.description: The service that is targeted by the event. For example: sshd.

columns.name: DestinationTranslatedAddress
columns.description: Identifies the translated destination referred to by the event in an IP network, as an IPv4 IP address.

columns.name: DestinationTranslatedPort
columns.description: Port after translation, such as a firewall Valid port numbers: 0 - 65535.

columns.name: DestinationUserID
columns.description: Identifies the destination user by ID. For example: in Unix, the root user is generally associated with the user ID 0.

columns.name: DestinationUserName
columns.description: Identifies the destination user by name.

columns.name: DestinationUserPrivileges
columns.description: Defines the destination use's privileges. Valid values: Admninistrator, User, Guest.

columns.name: DeviceAction

columns.name: DeviceAddress

columns.name: DeviceDnsDomain
columns.description: The DNS domain part of the full qualified domain name (FQDN).

columns.name: DeviceEventCategory
columns.description: Represents the category assigned by the originating device. Devices often use their own categorization schema to classify event. Example: '/Monitor/Disk/Read'.

columns.name: DeviceEventClassID
columns.description: String or integer that serves as a unique identifier per event type.

columns.name: DeviceExternalID
columns.description: A name that uniquely identifies the device generating the event.

columns.name: DeviceFacility
columns.description: The facility generating the event. For example: auth or local1.

columns.name: DeviceInboundInterface
columns.description: The interface on which the packet or data entered the device. For example: ethernet1/2.

columns.name: DeviceMacAddress
columns.description: The MAC address of the device generating the event.

columns.name: DeviceName
columns.description: The FQDN associated with the device node, when a node is available. For example: host.domain.com or host.

columns.name: DeviceNtDomain
columns.description: The Windows domain of the device address.

columns.name: DeviceOutboundInterface
columns.description: Interface on which the packet or data left the device.

columns.name: DevicePayloadId
columns.description: Unique identifier for the payload associated with the event.

columns.name: DeviceProduct
columns.description: String that together with device product and version definitions, uniquely identifies the type of sending device.

columns.name: DeviceTimeZone
columns.description: Timezone of the device generating the event.

columns.name: DeviceTranslatedAddress
columns.description: Identifies the translated device address that the event refers to, in an IP network. The format is an Ipv4 address.

columns.name: DeviceVendor
columns.description: String that together with device product and version definitions, uniquely identifies the type of sending device.

columns.name: DeviceVersion
columns.description: String that together with device product and version definitions, uniquely identifies the type of sending device.

columns.name: EndTime
columns.description: The time at which the activity related to the event ended.

columns.name: EventCount
columns.description: A count associated with the event, showing how many times the same event was observed.

columns.name: EventOutcome
columns.description: Displays the outcome, usually as 'success' or 'failure'.

columns.name: EventType
columns.description: Event type. Value values include: 0: base event, 1: aggregated, 2: correlation event, 3: action event. Note: This event can be omitted for base events.

columns.name: ExternalID
columns.description: Soon to be a deprecated field. Will be replaced by ExtID.

columns.name: ExtID
columns.description: An ID used by the originating device (will replace legacy ExternalID). Typically, these values have increasing values that are each associated with an event.

columns.name: FieldDeviceCustomNumber1
columns.description: One of three number fields available to map fields that do not apply to any other in this dictionary (will replace legacy DeviceCustomNumber1). Use sparingly and seek a more specific, dictionary supplied field when possible.

columns.name: FieldDeviceCustomNumber2
columns.description: One of three number fields available to map fields that do not apply to any other in this dictionary (will replace legacy DeviceCustomNumber2). Use sparingly and seek a more specific, dictionary supplied field when possible.

columns.name: FieldDeviceCustomNumber3
columns.description: One of three number fields available to map fields that do not apply to any other in this dictionary (will replace legacy DeviceCustomNumber3). Use sparingly and seek a more specific, dictionary supplied field when possible.

columns.name: FileCreateTime
columns.description: Time when the file was created.

columns.name: FileHash
columns.description: Hash of a file.

columns.name: FileID
columns.description: An ID associated with a file, such as the inode.

columns.name: FileModificationTime
columns.description: Time when the file was last modified.

columns.name: FileName
columns.description: The file's name, without the path.

columns.name: FilePath
columns.description: Full path to the file, including the filename. For example: C:\ProgramFiles\WindowsNT\Accessories\wordpad.exe or /usr/bin/zip.

columns.name: FilePermission
columns.description: The file's permissions. For example: '2,1,1'.

columns.name: FileSize
columns.description: The size of the file in bytes.

columns.name: FileType
columns.description: File type, such as pipe, socket, and so on.

columns.name: FlexDate1
columns.description: A timestamp field available to map a timestamp that does not apply to any other defined timestamp field in this dictionary. Use all flex fields sparingly and seek a more specific, dictionary supplied field when possible. These fields are typically reserved for customer use and should not be set by vendors unless necessary.

columns.name: FlexDate1Label
columns.description: The label field is a string and describes the purpose of the flex field.

columns.name: FlexNumber1
columns.description: Number fields available to map Int data that does not apply to any other field in this dictionary.

columns.name: FlexNumber1Label
columns.description: The label that describes the value in FlexNumber1

columns.name: FlexNumber2
columns.description: Number fields available to map Int data that does not apply to any other field in this dictionary.

columns.name: FlexNumber2Label
columns.description: The label that describes the value in FlexNumber2

columns.name: FlexString1
columns.description: One of four floating point fields available to map fields that do not apply to any other in this dictionary. Use sparingly and seek a more specific, dictionary supplied field when possible. These fields are typically reserved for customer use and should not be set by vendors unless necessary.

columns.name: FlexString1Label
columns.description: The label field is a string and describes the purpose of the flex field.

columns.name: FlexString2
columns.description: One of four floating point fields available to map fields that do not apply to any other in this dictionary. Use sparingly and seek a more specific, dictionary supplied field when possible. These fields are typically reserved for customer use and should not be set by vendors unless necessary.

columns.name: FlexString2Label
columns.description: The label field is a string and describes the purpose of the flex field.

columns.name: IndicatorThreatType
columns.description: The threat type of the MaliciousIP according to our TI feed.

columns.name: _IsBillable
columns.description: Specifies whether ingesting the data is billable. When _IsBillable isfalseingestion isn't billed to your Azure account

columns.name: LogSeverity
columns.description: A string or integer that describes the importance of the event. Valid string values: Unknown , Low, Medium, High, Very-High Valid integer values are: 0-3 = Low, 4-6 = Medium, 7-8 = High, 9-10 = Very-High.

columns.name: MaliciousIP
columns.description: If one of the IP in the message was correlate with the current TI feed we have it will show up here.

columns.name: MaliciousIPCountry
columns.description: The country of the MaliciousIP according to the GEO information at the time of the record ingestion.

columns.name: MaliciousIPLatitude
columns.description: The Latitude of the MaliciousIP according to the GEO information at the time of the record ingestion.

columns.name: MaliciousIPLongitude
columns.description: The Longitude of the MaliciousIP according to the GEO information at the time of the record ingestion.

columns.name: Message
columns.description: A message that gives more details about the event.

columns.name: OldFileCreateTime
columns.description: Time when the old file was created.

columns.name: OldFileHash
columns.description: Hash of the old file.

columns.name: OldFileID
columns.description: And ID associated with the old file, such as the inode.

columns.name: OldFileModificationTime
columns.description: Time when the old file was last modified.

columns.name: OldFileName
columns.description: Name of the old file.

columns.name: OldFilePath
columns.description: Full path to the old file, including the filename. For example: C:\ProgramFiles\WindowsNT\Accessories\wordpad.exe or /usr/bin/zip.

columns.name: OldFilePermission
columns.description: Permissions of the old file. For example: '2,1,1'.

columns.name: OldFileSize
columns.description: The size of the old file in bytes.

columns.name: OldFileType
columns.description: File type of the old file, such as a pipe, socket, and so on.

columns.name: OriginalLogSeverity
columns.description: A non-mapped version of LogSeverity. For example: Warning/Critical/Info insted of the normilized Low/Medium/High in the LogSeverity Field

columns.name: ProcessID
columns.description: Defines the ID of the process on the device generating the event.

columns.name: ProcessName
columns.description: Process name associated with the event. For example: in UNIX, the process generating the syslog entry.

columns.name: Protocol
columns.description: Transport protocol that identifies the Layer-4 protocol used. Possible values include protocol names, such as TCP or UDP.

columns.name: Reason
columns.description: The reason an audit event was generated. For example 'bad password' or 'unknown user'. This could also be an error or return code. Example: '0x1234'.

columns.name: ReceiptTime
columns.description: The time at which the event related to the activity was received. Different then the 'Timegenerated' field, which is when the event was recieved in the log collector machine.

columns.name: ReceivedBytes
columns.description: Number of bytes transferred inbound.

columns.name: RemoteIP
columns.description: The remote IP address, derived from the event's direction value, if possible.

columns.name: RemotePort
columns.description: The remote port, derived from the event's direction value, if possible.

columns.name: ReportReferenceLink
columns.description: Link to the report of the TI feed.

columns.name: RequestClientApplication
columns.description: The user agent associated with the request.

columns.name: RequestContext
columns.description: Describes the content from which the request originated, such as the HTTP Referrer.

columns.name: RequestCookies
columns.description: Cookies associated with the request.

columns.name: RequestMethod
columns.description: The method used to access a URL. Valid values include methods such as POST, GET, and so on.

columns.name: RequestURL
columns.description: The URL accessed for an HTTP request, including the protocol. For example: http://www/secure.com.

columns.name: _ResourceId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the resource that the record is associated with

columns.name: SentBytes
columns.description: Number of bytes transferred outbound.

columns.name: SimplifiedDeviceAction
columns.description: A mapped version of DeviceAction, such as Denied > Deny.

columns.name: SourceDnsDomain
columns.description: The DNS domain part of the complete FQDN.

columns.name: SourceHostName
columns.description: Identifies the source that event refers to in an IP network. Format should be a fully qualified domain name (DQDN) associated with the source node, when a node is available. For example: host or host.domain.com.

columns.name: SourceIP
columns.description: The source that an event refers to in an IP network, as an IPv4 address.

columns.name: SourceMACAddress
columns.description: Source MAC address.

columns.name: SourceNTDomain
columns.description: The Windows domain name for the source address.

columns.name: SourcePort
columns.description: The source port number. Valid port numbers are 0 - 65535.

columns.name: SourceProcessId
columns.description: The ID of the source process associated with the event.

columns.name: SourceProcessName
columns.description: The name of the event's source process.

columns.name: SourceServiceName
columns.description: The service responsible for generating the event.

columns.name: SourceSystem
columns.description: The type of agent the event was collected by. For example,OpsManagerfor Windows agent, either direct connect or Operations Manager,Linuxfor all Linux agents, orAzurefor Azure Diagnostics

columns.name: SourceTranslatedAddress
columns.description: Identifies the translated source that the event refers to in an IP network.

columns.name: SourceTranslatedPort
columns.description: Source port after translation, such as a firewall. Valid port numbers are 0 - 65535.

columns.name: SourceUserID
columns.description: Identifies the source user by ID.

columns.name: SourceUserName
columns.description: Identifies the source user by name. Email addresses are also mapped into the UserName fields. The sender is a candidate to put into this field.

columns.name: SourceUserPrivileges
columns.description: The source user's privileges. Valid values include: Administrator, User, Guest.

columns.name: StartTime
columns.description: The time when the activity that the event refers to started.

columns.name: _SubscriptionId

columns.name: ConfidenceLevel
columns.description: List of confidence levels of any spam or phishing verdicts. For spam, this column shows the spam confidence level (SCL), indicating if the email was skipped (-1), found to be not spam (0,1), found to be spam with moderate confidence (5,6), or found to be spam with high confidence (9). For phishing, this column displays whether the confidence level is "High" or "Low".

columns.name: DetectionMethods

columns.name: ActorName
columns.description: The UPN (User Principal Name) of the user who performed the action (specified in the Operation property) that resulted in the record being logged; for example, my_name@my_domain_name. Note that records for activity performed by system accounts (such as SHAREPOINT\system or NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM) are also included. In SharePoint, another value display in the UserId property is app@sharepoint. This indicates that the "user" who performed the activity was an application that has the necessary permissions in SharePoint to perform organization-wide actions (such as search a SharePoint site or OneDrive account) on behalf of a user, admin, or service. For more information, see the app@sharepoint user in audit records.

columns.name: ActorUserId
columns.description: An alternative ID for the user identified in the UserId property. For example, this property is populated with the passport unique ID (PUID) for events performed by users in SharePoint, OneDrive for Business, and Exchange. This property may also specify the same value as the UserID property for events occurring in other services and events performed by system accounts.

columns.name: ActorUserType
columns.description: The type of user that performed the operation. Possible types are: Admin, System, Application, Service Principal and Other.

For Dlp events, this can be "DlpRuleMatch", "DlpRuleUndo" or "DlpInfo", which are described under "DLP schema" below.

Also, for Azure Active Directory-related events, the IP address isn't logged and the value for the ClientIP property is null.
